# Unified Health Intelligence Report

**Date range:** 2025-03-08 to 2026-03-08  
**Generated:** 2026-03-08 15:15  
**Data sources active:** sleep, readiness, activity, heartrate, spo2, stress, workouts, body_composition, nutrition, mfp_weight  
**Total records analysed:** 4,951

---

## Nutrition (MyFitnessPal)

**Nutrition Logging Compliance:** 248/248 days (100%)

**Average Daily Calories:** 2,825 kcal

**Average Daily Macros:**

- Protein: 218g (31%)
- Carbs: 291g (41%)
- Fat: 89g (28%)

**Protein per kg bodyweight:** 2.5 g/kg (above 1.6–2.2g/kg target)

## Sleep & Recovery (Oura Ring)

**Average Sleep Score:** 87

![Weekly Sleep Score Trend](data:image/png;base64,)

Sleep scores have been **declining** over the period, ranging from 80 to 91 weekly average.

![Sleep Stage Breakdown](data:image/png;base64,)

Average nightly time spent in each sleep stage.

**Average Readiness Score:** 81

![Weekly Readiness Trend](data:image/png;base64,)

![Weekly HRV Trend](data:image/png;base64,)

HRV balance reflects heart rate variability relative to your personal baseline.

**Recovery patterns:** 246 high-readiness days (70%), 17 low-readiness days (5%) out of 349 total.

---

## Stress & Recovery Balance (Oura Ring)

**Average Daily High Stress:** 81 minutes
**Average Daily High Recovery:** 59 minutes
**Recovery:Stress Ratio:** 0.7:1

*Recovery time is lower than stress time — prioritise rest and recovery activities.*

![Weekly Stress vs Recovery](data:image/png;base64,)

Daily stress has been **increasing** over the past 28 days.

**Day Summary Distribution:**

- normal: 270 days (75%)
- stressful: 55 days (15%)
- restored: 25 days (7%)

## Training (Hevy)

**Total Sessions:** 125 (2.5/week)

![Weekly Training Volume](data:image/png;base64,)

Training volume has been **stable or declining** — first half avg 33,575 kg/week vs second half avg 33,114 kg/week.

![Muscle Group Distribution](data:image/png;base64,)

Volume distribution across muscle groups shows training emphasis.

**Progressive Overload (top exercises):**

- **Calf Extension (Machine)**: maintained at 130.0 kg
- **Leg Press (Machine)**: 160.0 kg → 200.0 kg (+40.0 kg)
- **Hack Squat (Machine)**: 60.0 kg → 70.0 kg (+10.0 kg)
- **Leg Extension (Machine)**: 65.0 kg → 70.0 kg (+5.0 kg)
- **Incline Bench Press (Dumbbell)**: 44.0 kg → 52.0 kg (+8.0 kg)

## Cross-Source Correlations & Insights

### Sleep vs Readiness

![Sleep vs Readiness](data:image/png;base64,)

A **strong positive correlation** (r = 0.65) — suggesting a meaningful relationship.

### Training Volume vs Next-Day Recovery

![Training Volume vs Next-Day Recovery](data:image/png;base64,)

A **weak positive correlation** (r = 0.11) — no strong linear relationship detected.

### Sleep vs Training Performance

![Sleep vs Training Performance](data:image/png;base64,)

A **weak positive correlation** (r = 0.01) — no strong linear relationship detected.

### Activity vs Next-Night Sleep

![Activity vs Next-Night Sleep](data:image/png;base64,)

A **weak negative correlation** (r = -0.08) — no strong linear relationship detected.

![Protein Intake vs Next-Day Recovery](data:image/png;base64,)

A **weak positive correlation** (r = 0.30) — no strong linear relationship detected.

A **weak negative correlation** (r = -0.14) — no strong linear relationship detected.

### Nutrition & Body Composition

Nutrition and body composition data are both available. Caloric balance trends can be compared against body composition scan changes to assess whether intake is tracking with weight/fat/muscle trends.

### Training & Body Composition

Training volume data and body composition scans are both available. Progressive overload trends can be compared against muscle mass changes.

---

## Alerts & Interventions

### 🟡 HRV Balance Declining

HRV balance has been trending downward — this often precedes illness or overtraining.

**Recommended actions:**

- Reduce training intensity (keep volume, lower load) for the next week
- Increase sleep opportunity by 30–60 minutes
- Check for early signs of illness (sore throat, fatigue)
- Ensure adequate micronutrient intake (vitamin D, zinc, magnesium)

### 🟡 Training Frequency Drop

Only 1 training session(s) in the last 14 days.

**Recommended actions:**

- If recovering from illness/injury, ease back with reduced volume
- If motivation is low, switch to shorter sessions (even 30 min counts)
- Consistency matters more than intensity — 3×/week minimum recommended

### 🟡 Training Volume Declining

Weekly volume dropped from ~20,120 kg to ~7,301 kg over the last 8 weeks (-64%).

**Recommended actions:**

- If intentional (deload), this is fine — plan to ramp back up
- If unintentional, review programming — are you progressing or stalling?
- Check recovery markers (sleep, readiness) — under-recovery limits training capacity

### 🟡 Push/Pull Imbalance

Push volume is 1.5× pull volume — ideally this should be close to 1:1.

**Recommended actions:**

- Add more rowing and pulling movements (cable rows, face pulls, pull-ups)
- Aim for equal sets of horizontal push and pull per week
- Imbalance increases shoulder injury risk over time

### 🔵 Undertrained Muscle Groups

These groups receive less than 3% of total volume: biceps, adductors, lower_back, full_body.

**Recommended actions:**

- Add targeted accessory work for biceps, adductors, lower_back, full_body
- Even 2–3 sets per week can prevent imbalances

*Alert summary: 🟡 4 medium | 🔵 1 low*

---

## Summary — Top Observations

1. Sleep quality is excellent (avg score 87) — a strong foundation for recovery.
2. Training frequency averages 2.5 sessions/week across 125 total sessions.
3. Notable sleep-readiness correlation (r=0.65) — worth monitoring.
4. Muscle mass change: +4.1 kg over the period.
